print_info.print_location: ศูนย์สื่อและสิ่งพิมพ์แก้วเจ้าจอม มหาวิทยาลัยราชภัฏสวนสุนันทา

warnings: All figures transcribed from Thai numerals (๐-๙) on a grayscale/low-contrast printed table -- individual digit misreads are possible despite reasonable confidence; recommend spot-checking against the source if these figures feed into any downstream cost model.

House #6's row shows ฐานรากแผ่ (spread footing, 1,227,000 baht) MORE EXPENSIVE than ฐานรากเสาเข็ม (pile foundation, 1,102,000 baht) -- the reverse of every other row on this table (pile foundation is consistently pricier elsewhere, as expected since piling adds cost). This exact same anomaly pattern (one house's pricing reversed) was also flagged in house #3's equivalent reference table for a different house number -- worth a consolidated human review across all houses' pricing tables rather than treating each as an isolated one-off.

House #10's pile-foundation cost (2,945,000) is disproportionately higher than its spread-footing cost (2,035,000) -- a ~45% gap vs the ~5-10% gap seen in every other row -- flagged as a likely transcription-worthy anomaly (possibly a genuine outlier for a larger/heavier design, or a digit misread of 2,145,000 or similar; not resolved here).
